title: Claude 2026 Prompting and Claude Code Practice · Failure Triage
summary: Apply current Anthropic guidance to tool use, examples, context, and long-running coding. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Claude-specific prompt and eval suite and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use clear direct instructions, motivation, and relevant context for current Claude models.
Use diverse examples and structural tags when they improve task boundary recognition.
Steer tool selection with descriptions and instructions, using enforced tool choice only when required.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: prompt claude practice. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-claude-code-context-flmh0e" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Claude Code Context into observable performance evidence.

Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Claude Code Context applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide Claude Code repository guidance, verification commands, permissions, and durable project context.
